retrieved_docs.title: Electrophysiology Study and Ablation of Atrial Tachycardia -- Complications -- Atrioesophageal fistula
retrieved_docs.content: This complication is specific to LA ablation. Although rare, ablation in the posterior left atrium near the esophagus can cause a serious complication known as gastroesophageal fistula. This medical emergency can result in air or food entering the heart chambers, causing sepsis and often requiring surgical intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: Bariatric Surgery Cardiac Outcomes -- Clinical Significance -- Atrial Fibrillation
retrieved_docs.content: Atrial fibrillation is associated with risk factors such as diabetes mellitus, hypertension, obstructive sleep apnea, and obesity. Recent studies have shown promise for bariatric surgery as an intervention to reduce the risk of atrial fibrillation in individuals with obesity. [7] However, a secondary analysis of the Look AHEAD trial indicated that nonsurgical weight loss did not significantly decrease the incidence of atrial fibrillation. [16] Furthermore, a 2019 meta-analysis provided limited evidence supporting a reduced occurrence of atrial fibrillation after nonsurgical weight loss. Interestingly, the same meta-analysis revealed that even a modest weight gain of 5% is linked to a 13% increase in the incidence of atrial fibrillation. [17] Implementing preventive measures to prevent weight gain in the general population is imperative to alleviate the global burden of atrial fibrillation.

retrieved_docs.title: Laparoscopic Gastric Bypass -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: The patient is given preoperative antibiotics 30 minutes before incision as well as venous thromboembolism prophylaxis. Abdominal hair is removed with clippers in the preoperative area. After induction of anesthesia, a Foley catheter is inserted, and an orogastric tube is positioned within the stomach.

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Head and Neck: Anterior Vagus Nerve -- Surgical Considerations -- Fundoplication
retrieved_docs.content: Investigators have experimented with severing the hepatic branch of the anterior vagal trunk while performing a fundoplication to determine the post-operative effects on the function of the gallbladder. This branch coming off the anterior vagal trunk innervates the liver, biliary apparatus, and provides motor innervation to the gallbladder (i.e., gallbladder contractile function). [10] Purdy et al. studied this because laparoscopic fundoplications were considered technically easier with the cutting of this branch, but limited data about postoperative effects on general gallbladder motor function was available. They cite a Finnish surgeon, Dr. M. Paakkonen, who altered the technique of handling the hepatic branch during fundoplications in 2001. Patients who had fundoplications done before and after this date received symptom questionnaires; from these patients, 19 were investigated further in two distinct groups. In particular, the volume of the gallbladder and gallbladder ejection fraction after a fatty meal (measured by ultrasound) were of importance, as well as the serum concentrations of alkaline phosphatase, alanine aminotransferase, total bilirubin, and amylase. The investigators noted no significant difference in symptoms or PRN use of medication between the two groups. [10] [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Surgical Access Incisions -- Issues of Concern -- Incisions for Robotic Surgery
retrieved_docs.content: The first robotic mitral valve repair was performed in 1998 by Alain Carpentier using a Da Vinci system, which has subsequently been used for bypass grafting, valve repair, and congenital defects, in addition to more complex procedures such as a 2-patch repair of a right partial anomalous pulmonary venous connection and repair of a sinus venosus atrial septal defect. Robotic-assisted thoracic surgery has shown potential advantages, including more effective lymph node dissection in oncological procedures. [70] [71] Transoral robotic surgery, first reported in 2005, has become a standard approach for treating tumors of the tongue base, oropharynx, hypopharynx, parapharyngeal space, and supraglottic larynx and addressing obstructive sleep apnea. The Da Vinci system is primarily used for these procedures, offering precise and minimally invasive access to difficult-to-reach areas. [72] [73]

Urological: total cystectomy Adrenal resection It is worth noting that procedures involving a laparoscopic approach may be associated with cardiovascular complications, so these patients should undergo a preoperative risk assessment. retrieved_docs.title: Colon Resection (Archived) -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Anastomotic leak is a dreaded complication for all surgeons performing colonic resection and primary anastomosis. A high index of suspicion is indicated if postoperatively, a patient does not progress as expected, as signs and symptoms can be subtle to start with, especially if the leak is in the pelvis and the patient has a defunctioning stoma. Patients may have pyrexia, tachycardia, cardiac arrhythmias (new-onset atrial fibrillation), complain of abdominal pain and bloating, and have a distended, tender, and potentially peritonitic abdomen. Inflammatory markers will be raised more markedly than expected in the postoperative period. Free air under the diaphragm may not be visible on an erect chest x-ray, but CT will show pneumoperitoneum and fluid around the anastomosis. In patients who have undergone anterior resection, it is advised to perform the scan with rectal contrast.

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Head and Neck: Anterior Vagus Nerve -- Surgical Considerations -- Fundoplication
retrieved_docs.content: A common and unfortunate post-operative side effect of this procedure is flatulence. A report published in September of 2019 by Cockbain et al. investigated the incidence of post-operative flatulence in patients who underwent primary laparoscopic fundoplication. The study entitled Flatulence After Anti-reflux Treatment (FAART) identified select patients who had this procedure done for symptomatic gastroesophageal reflux disease refractory to medical therapy from 1999 and 2009, and scrutinized incidence of flatulence at follow-up. At a follow-up of 8 to 15 years post-op, 85% of patients reported excessive flatulence. The study also concluded that flatulence was indeed worse in patients who had a total fundoplication in contrast to those who underwent a partial fundoplication. Although the incidence in this population was high, the overall impact on daily life was small. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 1 -- Community 218
retrieved_docs.content: disease (مرض قلبي روماتزمي) are commonly associated with atrial fibrillation (الرجفان الأذيني) and ventricular tachycardia. Specific entities include paroxysmal atrial tachycardia (with and without block), postoperative complete heart block, unspecified heart block, and other specified rhythm disorders (Overige gespecificeerde hartritmestoornissen). Conduction abnormalities often localize to the atrioventricular node (nodus atrioventricularis) and may present with bradycardia or non‑sinus rhythm on electrocardiographic recordings. Electro‑diagnostic evaluation ranges from standard ECG monitoring to advanced modalities such as GSPECT myocardial imaging with technetium‑99m sestamibi, microvolt T‑wave alternans, and carotid sinus stimulation with simultaneous ECG. Invasive cardiac electrophysiology studies—including arrhythmia induction, arrhythmogenic focus ablation, and targeted treatment of atrial fibrillation, supraventricular tachycardia, and ventricular tachycardia—address the

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2557
retrieved_docs.content: The electrophysiologic evaluation with arrhythmia induction, arrhythmogenic focus ablation, treatment of atrial fibrillation and the electrophysiologic evaluation with arrhythmia induction, arrhythmogenic focus ablation, treatment of supraventricular tachycardia are both classified as a دراسة فيزيولوجية كهربائية قلبية (cardiac electrophysiological study). Each procedure involves deliberately inducing arrhythmias, identifying the arrhythmogenic focus, performing ablation of that focus, and delivering therapeutic treatment targeted to a specific rhythm disorder—atrial fibrillation in the first case and supraventricular tachycardia in the second. As forms of cardiac electrophysiological study, they provide essential diagnostic insight and therapeutic options for managing abnormal heart rhythms.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2689
retrieved_docs.content: The medical procedure identified as “Operation on esophageal varices” is described as having the morphology “وريد متدلي,” indicating that the target anatomical feature is a hanging or dilated vein. Similarly, the specific technique termed “ربط دوالي المريء” (ligation of esophageal varices) is directly characterized by the same morphology “وريد متدلي.” Both the general operation on esophageal varices and the ligation approach share this morphological description, emphasizing that the underlying pathological structure involved in these interventions is a dilated esophageal vein.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 2190
retrieved_docs.content: Plication is a surgical technique that encompasses several specific procedures, including plication of the kidney, suture plication of an artery for control of gastric hemorrhage, and ureteroplication. In plication of the kidney, renal tissue is folded or sutured to reduce kidney volume or correct structural abnormalities. Suture plication of an artery for control of gastric hemorrhage involves applying sutures to an arterial vessel to achieve hemostasis during severe gastric bleeding. Ureteroplication refers to the folding or suturing of the ureter to address conditions such as reflux or obstruction. All of these variants share the underlying principle of tissue manipulation to achieve therapeutic goals in different anatomical regions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 2497
retrieved_docs.content: OPERATIONS AND ANESTHESIA PROCEDURES is categorized under منظار داخلي (internal endoscopy), indicating that endoscopic techniques are considered a parent framework for many operative and anesthetic interventions. The Arabic term جراحة (surgery) is also linked to منظار داخلي as a sub‑category, reflecting the close association between surgical practice and endoscopic methods. Diagnostic procedures on nose, mouth and pharynx are specifically related to منظار داخلي, emphasizing that endoscopic examination is a key tool for evaluating these upper‑airway regions, and these diagnostic procedures are further classified under عمليات الأنف (nose surgeries), showing that they belong to the broader surgical domain of nasal procedures. Clinically, جراحة is associated with عمليات الأنف, underscoring that nasal surgeries are a distinct subset of general surgical care. Within the surgical hierarchy, ultrazvuková chirurgie (ultrasound‑guided surgery) is a child of جراحة, illustrating that advanced imaging‑assisted techniques are nested under the general surgery category. Finally, Postoperative complete heart block is noted to occur after جراحة, highlighting that this serious cardiac conduction complication can arise as a postoperative sequela of any surgical operation, including those performed endoscopically or with ultrasound guidance. Together, these relationships depict a structured network in which endoscopic methods, general and specialized surgeries, and their diagnostic and postoperative considerations are interlinked for comprehensive patient care.
